283204aaa3cb.description: Close losing positions proactively with high confidence (0.8-0.9) to free margin and limit drawdowns. Multiple agents demonstrated this pattern.

550cdce39bab.description: Explicit validation step ('Validate_trade cleared the trade') before execution improves outcomes. agentic_gptoss used this but still lost -$26.29, suggesting validation criteria need refinement for mixed markets.

86ce05212674.description: Trade frequency should adapt to market regime: moderate bull markets require 0-30 trades/24h maximum. Above 100 trades correlates with losses (-$50 to -$264).

30310e25755c.description: Close short positions immediately when market shows consistent positive returns across all assets. Holding shorts in +1-2% market destroys capital.

186df825d306.description: Explicit validation step before trade execution does NOT prevent losses if regime assessment is wrong. agentic_gptoss used validation but still lost -$50.58.

151d3979c854.description: 2% equity risk with 2:1 reward ratio fails when directionally wrong. skill_aware_oss used this framework but lost -$173.25 on wrong-direction shorts.

c61e4c3ceede.description: Trade frequency should adapt to market regime: moderate bull markets require 0-25 trades/24h maximum. ta_baseline (23 trades, -$28.69) vs llama4_scout (243 trades, -$229.00).

8a1f893153e7.description: Position sizing at 2% equity risk with 2:1 reward ratio fails when directionally wrong. skill_aware_oss used this framework but lost $-167.78 by shorting in bull market.

fceed55ea5bb.description: Explicit validation step ('Validate_trade approved', 'validation passed') does NOT prevent losses if regime assessment is wrong. agentic_gptoss used validation but lost $-55.63.

f2130a861be6.description: Trade frequency must adapt to market volatility: flat markets (<0.1% daily moves) require 0-20 trades/24h maximum. Exceeding this threshold guarantees losses to transaction costs.

d5187cdeac0d.description: Position sizing at 2% equity risk with 2:1 reward ratio fails when market lacks directional movement. skill_aware_oss used this approach but lost -$183.75 in flat market.

67e888ac3e1e.description: Close positions near breakeven to free margin ONLY when higher-conviction opportunity is immediately available. Without clear opportunity, holding is superior to closing.

a07176433a20.description: Explicit validation step before trade execution does NOT prevent losses if market regime assessment is wrong. Both agentic_gptoss and skill_aware_oss validated trades but lost heavily.

d7604d616d18.description: In flat markets, transaction costs dominate returns. Each trade costs ~0.1-0.2% round-trip, making profitability impossible with >50 trades when market moves <0.1%.

635e36b7507e.description: Trade frequency must adapt to market regime: bearish markets (all assets -2% to -6%) require 0-5 trades/24h maximum. Each additional trade in bearish conditions increases losses.

61391536a7cf.description: Position sizing at 2% equity risk with 2:1 reward ratio fails when directionally wrong in bearish markets. skill_aware_oss used explicit risk calculator but still lost -$156.03.

ea7ba19f2fef.description: Close positions proactively when market shows consistent negative returns across all assets. gptoss_20b_simple's reasoning 'closing now locks the small loss and frees margin' is correct approach but insufficient without stopping new entries.

e7130d55ea0d.description: In bearish markets, transaction costs compound losses. High-frequency traders (189-208 trades) lost 3-4x more than low-frequency traders (24-65 trades) beyond just directional losses.

e5a1f48a155d.description: Close losing positions immediately when multi-timeframe signals conflict with actual price action. skill_aware_oss correctly closed SOLUSDT citing 'conflicting bullish/bearish trends' but continued opening new positions.

1ec110499faa.description: Position sizing at 2% equity risk with 2:1 reward ratio SUCCEEDS when directionally aligned with market regime. skill_aware_oss used this approach for shorts in bearish market, achieving +$930.12.

83d400f82335.description: Explicit validation step ('validation passed', 'risk calculator gives excellent 2:1 reward-to-risk') SUCCEEDS when combined with correct directional bias. skill_aware_oss validated all trades and profited.

87a4c3d9c3c9.description: Close losing positions immediately with high confidence (0.85-0.95) when market regime contradicts position direction. skill_only_oss: 'Closing the position preserves capital and aligns with zero-trading strategy in bearish conditions' (0.95 confidence).

7d2db5a25fcf.description: Concentration warnings ('concentration warning only') should NOT prevent trades when multi-timeframe alignment is strong. skill_aware_oss proceeded despite warnings and profited.

b1f2b6fb637d.description: Trade frequency must adapt to market regime: strong bearish markets allow 100-180 trades/24h IF short-biased, but require 0 trades if long-biased.

8e011ab9eaac.description: Cutting losing shorts when funding rate is positive ('avoid paying positive funding on the short position') preserves capital. gptoss_20b_simple used this rule effectively.

9801576a5cdc.description: Close losing positions immediately when funding costs compound losses. gptoss_120b_simple correctly closed BTC short citing 'losing and incurring funding costs' with 0.80 confidence.

4f6340e7d973.description: Position sizing at 2% equity risk with 2:1 reward ratio FAILS when directional bias is wrong or market is choppy. skill_aware_oss used this framework but lost -$627.15.

78623af867ee.description: Explicit validation step ('validation passes', 'risk calculator shows excellent') does NOT prevent losses if market regime assessment is incorrect. skill_aware_oss had validation on all trades but was worst performer.

ee4b99cd8048.description: Trade frequency inversely correlates with performance in bearish markets: 2-6 trades = breakeven, 128 trades = +$92, 157+ trades = massive losses.

38880aa69f60.description: Closing positions to 'free margin for better opportunities' is valid when executed with high confidence (0.90+) and clear loss-cutting rationale.

536df1fe3dfd.description: Concentration warnings should trigger position reduction. agentic_gptoss closed ETH citing 'high concentration' and achieved positive returns.

81747101422f.description: Trade frequency must adapt to market regime: mixed/low-volatility markets require 0-10 trades/24h maximum. Agents with 100+ trades all lost significantly.

aaf132efedf6.description: Position sizing at 2% equity risk with 2:1 reward ratio FAILS when market direction is unclear. skill_aware_oss used this approach and lost -$730.26.

4075c2c67fee.description: Explicit validation step ('validation passed', 'risk calculator shows excellent') does NOT prevent losses if market regime assessment is wrong.

62556bc18f84.description: Closing losing positions quickly with high confidence (0.85-0.90) and explicit reasoning ('limits further downside', 'frees margin') reduces but does not eliminate losses.

3d98e7889e05.description: Concentration warnings should trigger position reduction or closure. gptoss_120b_simple: 'frees margin for other opportunities' - still lost but less than concentrated positions.

fdb9b2823760.description: In low-volatility mixed markets, transaction costs compound losses. High-frequency traders (100+ trades) lost 5-15x more than low-frequency traders.

2c661d761e57.description: Trade frequency sweet spot in mixed/slightly bullish markets is 24-158 trades/24h. Below 24 may miss opportunities, above 158 incurs excessive transaction costs and directional errors.

2f61dbfdc968.description: Close positions to 'free margin' when profitable, not when losing. gptoss_20b_simple closed profitable BNB short to reduce risk and achieved +$161.80.

f5522fa1cf15.description: Margin utilization warnings (>90%) should trigger immediate position reduction. skill_aware_oss closed SOL at 92% margin utilization, contributing to positive PnL.

dd82d1c31376.description: Position sizing at 2% equity risk with 2:1 reward ratio FAILS when market direction is misread. agentic_gptoss consistently cited this framework but lost $391.94.

3ac7dd891cd9.description: High leverage (5x-10x) with 'optimal' risk calculator validation does NOT protect against directional errors. agentic_gptoss used 5x-10x leverage with validation and still lost significantly.

3daaa0d87ab6.description: Closing losing positions quickly with explicit reasoning ('limits further drawdown', 'preserve capital') is valid when executed promptly. qwen3_235b closed losing ETH longs but still lost -$126.88 due to late execution.

49533d1d0aff.description: Trade frequency inversely correlates with performance in mixed markets: 0-40 trades = profitable or flat, 138-227 trades = significant losses (-$103 to -$396).

68aa2f466404.description: High leverage (5x) with 'optimal' risk calculator validation does NOT protect against directional errors. gptoss_120b_simple used 5x leverage with validation, lost $103.93.

aa54c6711b47.description: Closing positions to 'cut losses and free margin' should only be done when the position contradicts the prevailing market trend, not as a general loss-cutting mechanism.

33fb33c9c60e.description: Multi-timeframe conflict and overbought RSI as exit signals show moderate success. skill_aware_oss used this reasoning to close positions, limiting losses to -$24.75 despite 148 trades.

13e5a8c1a925.description: Trade frequency inversely correlates with performance in bullish markets: 8 trades = $0.00, 30-34 trades = -$46 to -$51, 120-166 trades = -$41 to -$135

72cb48fcaaf5.description: Position sizing at 2% equity risk with 2:1 reward ratio FAILS when transaction costs exceed expected edge. All agents using this framework lost money despite correct directional bias

23f638db6d0a.description: High leverage (4x-5x) with 'optimal' risk calculator validation does NOT protect against overtrading losses. skill_aware_oss used 4x leverage with 2% risk and still lost $81.93

4c0d68f3ffc9.description: Multi-timeframe alignment (15m, 1h, 4h) as entry confirmation leads to excessive trading. Agents citing this reasoning averaged 149 trades/24h vs 19 trades for non-citing agents

7bc7f8ae743f.description: In uniformly bearish markets, 'low-severity concentration warning, proceed with caution' on SHORT positions is ACCEPTABLE. skill_aware_oss acknowledged warning but proceeded, achieving +$691.44.

6182b7a05000.description: Position sizing at 2% equity risk with 2:1 reward ratio SUCCEEDS when market direction is correctly identified. skill_aware_oss: 'risk-reward 2:1 with excellent risk calc' on shorts in bearish market = profitable.

1296a66e6e22.description: Closing SHORT positions 'to lock in gains' when profitable is correct behavior in bearish markets. gptoss_20b_simple: 'Close the small profitable short to lock in gains' - taking profits works.

074e268b63d1.description: Closing positions due to 'conflicting timeframes' when market is uniformly directional reduces returns. agentic_gptoss closed profitable shorts prematurely due to perceived conflicts.

6b3fe73c8397.description: Low leverage (2x) on SHORT positions with positive funding rate is optimal in bearish markets. gptoss_20b_simple: 'funding rate is positive, so shorts receive funding' - correct reasoning.

7a67377536cc.description: Position sizing at 2% equity risk with 2:1 reward ratio SUCCEEDS when agent maintains conviction through short-term adverse moves. skill_aware_oss held shorts through BTC's +1.57% move and still profited overall.

a85f74c4a24a.description: Closing positions 'to reduce concentration' without clear re-entry criteria leads to churn losses. agentic_gptoss closed then re-opened BTC shorts multiple times, paying spread/fees each cycle.

f147a6dd586d.description: Multi-timeframe bearish alignment (15m, 1h, 4h) as SHORT entry signal has mixed results in mixed-bullish markets. Works for skill_aware_oss (+$409) but fails for agentic_gptoss (-$619) - execution discipline matters more than signal.

8b706408231a.description: Cutting small losses quickly (-$0.14 to -$21) to 'free margin' destroys edge. gptoss_20b_simple's premature exits prevented recovery on valid setups.

e679eb37b3e1.description: High trade frequency (165-172 trades/24h) can be profitable IF position management is disciplined. Same frequency produces +$409 (skill_aware_oss) vs -$619 (agentic_gptoss).

db99b9426e95.description: Validation checks passing ('all checks passed', 'validation confirms') does not guarantee profitability. agentic_gptoss and gptoss_20b_simple both cite validation approval but have negative PnL.

46ac3b8f019f.description: Position sizing at 2% equity risk with 2:1 reward ratio SUCCEEDS when agent maintains SHORT bias in bearish markets and uses validation to confirm market direction alignment.

29b1997e3f42.description: Cutting small losses quickly (-$0.01 to -$21) to 'free margin' destroys edge. gptoss_20b_simple lost $189.24 with this approach while market moved 10-16% in favorable direction for shorts.

83bbf791f188.description: Closing positions 'to reduce concentration' or 'reduce exposure' without clear re-entry criteria leads to whipsaw losses. Agents closed then re-entered same direction, paying spread/fees twice.

4eaff07ebb59.description: High trade frequency (183 trades/24h) can be profitable IF position management prioritizes 'locking in gains' over 'cutting losses'. skill_aware_oss: +$1682.05 vs agentic_gptoss: -$246.40 with identical trade counts.

90669041f1b5.description: Multi-timeframe bearish alignment (15m, 1h, 4h) as SHORT entry signal SUCCEEDS in uniformly bearish markets. skill_aware_oss used this for +$2911.52 profit.

019c3b472dbb.description: Position sizing at 2% equity risk with 2:1 reward ratio SUCCEEDS when market direction aligns with position bias and agent maintains high trade frequency (150-200/24h).

780acfde9f5b.description: Closing LONG positions quickly to pivot to trend-aligned SHORTs preserves capital. 'Close existing SOL long before initiating ETH short' is correct behavior.

18f984d0bed7.description: High trade frequency (189-200 trades/24h) can be profitable IF position management prioritizes trend alignment over risk reduction. skill_aware_oss outperformed agentic_gptoss by 22x.

016ed7bfa43f.description: Low trade frequency (<20 trades/24h) in strongly trending markets destroys edge. ta_baseline (19 trades: -$99) and gpt_simple (6 trades: -$163) both lost money.

49ee40fab677.description: Position sizing at 2% equity risk with 2:1 reward ratio FAILS when market direction opposes trade. skill_aware_oss used this framework consistently while losing -$963. Risk management cannot save directionally wrong trades.

0573740f51de.description: Low trade frequency (<40 trades/24h) preserves capital when agent bias conflicts with market. ta_baseline (38 trades, -$120) and gpt_simple (10 trades, -$140) vs skill_aware_oss (166 trades, -$963).

b4d3fc85a49b.description: Validation checks passing ('all checks passed', 'validation confirms') does NOT guarantee profitable trades. All losing agents passed validation while making wrong-direction trades.

d43d419336f2.description: Cutting small losses quickly (-$38 on SOL) to 'free margin for future trades' destroys edge when market continues against original position. gptoss_20b_simple closed SOL short at -$38 loss while SOL gained +3.38%.

63ded77865cb.description: High concentration warnings (127% of equity in ETH shorts) correctly identified by skill_aware_oss but closing to 'reduce concentration' then re-entering shorts negated the risk reduction.

153a948a24b6.description: Quick loss-cutting on contrarian positions preserves capital for next opportunity. glm_simple repeatedly closed losing SOL longs at 0.85-0.90 confidence when trend continued against position.

dd8a578f454d.description: High trade frequency (137 trades/24h) CAN be profitable with disciplined position management and quick exits. glm_simple's 60% decision-to-trade ratio with +$116.50 profit.

196197184d08.description: Medium trade frequency (36-38 trades/24h) with wrong directional bias destroys capital. Both glm_agentic (-$100.02) and glm_skill_aware (-$29.48) had similar trade counts but wrong market read.

6184e95f0efa.description: Closing positions 'to lock in profits' at small gains (+$2.85) while market continues in favorable direction is suboptimal but preserves realized gains.

c0644cc1946c.description: Lock profits at 3-4% gains when funding rate contradicts position direction. gptoss_simple closed BTC short at 3.9% profit citing negative funding (crowded shorts).

0eea8f25a1e4.description: High-confidence ML predictions (97%) should be treated as contrarian signals in mixed markets. Kronos 97% bearish predictions preceded bullish moves.

829cf83c0026.description: 2% equity risk with 2:1 reward ratio FAILS when market regime is misidentified. gptoss_agentic and gptoss_skill_aware both used this sizing but lost due to wrong directional bias.

c5aa5b4ccd22.description: Trade frequency sweet spot in mixed markets is 4-20 trades/24h. Below 4 misses opportunities, above 20 increases exposure to regime misreads.

640d397989af.description: Self-reflective position management (recognizing overtrading on specific symbols and closing to diversify) outperforms mechanical risk rules. gptoss_journal_aware's meta-cognitive exits led to +$187.76 vs -$720 to -$754 for agents with sophisticated but non-reflective risk frameworks.

a790a9ff479e.description: Position churn (repeatedly opening and closing the same directional trade on the same symbol) destroys edge even when direction is correct. gptoss_forum_aware's DOGE short churn lost money despite DOGE falling -1.05%.

8867cd487ab9.description: 2% equity risk with 2:1 reward ratio FAILS when combined with regime misidentification AND high trade frequency. gptoss_skill_aware used this sizing on 65 trades with wrong regime read, losing -$376.08. The same sizing worked for gptoss_agentic (+$262.43) who had better directional accuracy.

9d52a5847f25.description: Scaling into winning positions with concentration warnings acknowledged but overridden can work. gptoss_agentic scaled BTC shorts 'despite low-severity concentration warning' and profited, suggesting concentration is acceptable when directional conviction is validated by price action.

d4d7b91d616d.description: Closing positions citing funding rate costs ('shorts pay funding', 'positive funding') is a valid risk management signal. gptoss20b_simple closed BTC short citing positive funding to avoid costs — prudent capital preservation even if it limited upside.

18adb6d7ad11.description: When ALL 5 tracked assets are positive over 24h, immediately halt all SHORT entries regardless of lower-timeframe signals. This single rule would have prevented $3681 in losses across 4 agents.

e665d9be32e9.description: Trade frequency must be inversely proportional to regime uncertainty. In this window, agents making 2-4 trades lost $0-$29, while agents making 37-56 trades lost $500-$1160. Reduce frequency when conviction should be low.

4c184726937f.description: 2% equity risk with 2:1 reward ratio FAILS catastrophically when combined with regime misidentification AND high trade frequency. gptoss_skill_aware used this sizing on 53 trades with wrong regime read, losing -$720.27. The risk framework amplifies losses when direction is wrong.

83927551c7c5.description: Position churn on same symbol same direction continues to destroy edge. Multiple agents repeatedly opened and closed shorts on BTC and ETH, paying spread/fees each time while the underlying moved against them.

2986bc145c56.description: Self-reflective position management (journal_aware closing to reduce exposure) limited losses to -$501.98 vs -$1109 to -$1160 for non-reflective high-frequency agents. Self-awareness helps but cannot overcome fundamental directional error.

a84b952725dd.description: When ALL 5 tracked assets are positive over 24h with gains exceeding +7%, immediately halt ALL SHORT entries regardless of any lower-timeframe signals, validation checks, or risk calculators. This single rule would have prevented ~$1908 in losses across 3 agents.

4a16ec6ce06c.description: Trade frequency must be inversely proportional to regime uncertainty. In this window: 0 trades = $0, 5 trades = +$40, 42-53 trades = -$434 to -$754. When conviction about regime is low, reduce frequency dramatically.

5d6186b57bf4.description: Validation checks passing ('excellent 2:1 risk/reward', 'validation passed with no violations') provide FALSE confidence when directional bias is wrong. Every validated short entry in this window lost money.

5daed15ec851.description: In uniformly bearish markets, agents with 5 or fewer trades and self-reflective management outperform. journal_aware (5 trades, +$920.88) and agentic (5 trades, +$282.25) vs skill_aware (5 trades, -$0.00) and gptoss20b_simple (4 trades, -$1.03).

21b5a5f69660.description: Self-reflective position management (journal-aware) continues to outperform in bearish markets. +$920.88 is the best single-agent performance this window, consistent with prior pattern of journal-aware leading in various regimes.

fb6601b68e8a.description: In bearish markets, cap trade frequency at 15 trades/24h maximum. Agents with 10-13 trades profited (+$56 to +$1885) while agents with 40-46 trades lost (-$433 to -$444). The relationship is stark: more trades = more losses even when direction is correct.

af5e20375757.description: Position churn (repeatedly opening and closing same direction on same symbol) destroys edge even in trending markets. High-frequency agents likely paid excessive spread/fees and got stopped out on intraday noise before the trend resumed.

dc6301af834f.description: Self-reflective position management (journal-aware) does NOT guarantee outperformance. In this window, journal-aware lost -$443.88 (worst performer), contradicting prior windows where it was the best performer. Self-reflection may lead to premature exits in trending markets.

8a1623aee318.description: Zero trading or minimal trading preserves capital when uncertain. Three agents with 0 trades had $0 PnL, avoiding all losses in a -2% to -6% market.

e47f21eb2629.description: In mildly bullish markets with small daily moves (<2%), cap trade frequency at 10 trades/24h maximum. ta_bot's 8 trades = +$25.04 vs skill_aware's 52 trades = -$222.14.

0f9221d2e87d.description: 2% equity risk with 2:1 reward ratio FAILS when combined with high trade frequency (52 trades) and regime misidentification. gptoss_skill_aware lost -$222.14 despite using this 'validated' sizing framework.

4144d3c9c470.description: Zero trading preserves capital perfectly when market direction is uncertain. 5 agents with 0 trades had $0 PnL, avoiding all losses.

4ab1034ced6d.description: Take profit at meaningful ROE levels (10%+) rather than holding through potential reversals. ta_bot closed SOLUSDT at 10.3% ROE, contributing to its +$25.04 profit.

2cdb7b0a6ac2.description: In mixed/low-volatility markets (daily moves < 1.5%), zero trading or ultra-low frequency (0-1 trades) preserves capital. 6 out of 11 agents made 0-1 trades and had $0 PnL, outperforming 2 of 3 active traders.

ff219e54401b.description: Self-reflective position management (journal-aware) can enable profitable high-frequency trading even in mixed markets. journal_aware's 69 trades yielded +$237.32 while non-reflective agents with fewer trades lost money.

c9cf0694e0da.description: When ALL 5 tracked assets are positive over 24h with gains exceeding +3%, immediately halt ALL SHORT entries regardless of any lower-timeframe signals, validation checks, or risk calculators. This single rule would have prevented most losses across 5 losing agents.

51b80923876c.description: Cap trade frequency at 12 trades/24h maximum in bullish markets when agent bias is uncertain. gptoss_forum_aware's 12 trades (+$40.06) vs gptoss_journal_aware's 57 trades (-$87.83) demonstrates the frequency-loss relationship.

74fa31a91cc1.description: Low trade frequency (6-8 trades) with wrong directional bias still loses money but limits damage. Qwen agents lost -$52 to -$66 on 6-8 trades vs journal_aware losing -$87.83 on 57 trades. Wrong direction is the primary problem; frequency amplifies it.

cde22f670b49.description: Index fund passive LONG allocation in uniformly bullish markets should show positive PnL. index_fund's $0 reported PnL despite holding longs through +3.3% to +6.25% gains suggests either positions were not held or PnL reporting is delayed/unrealized.

ecc2cf8e56d7.description: In moderately bearish markets (daily moves -0.4% to -6.2%), zero trading preserves capital. 6/8 agents with 0 trades had $0 PnL vs 2 active agents losing -$51 to -$172.

10812a05e0d8.description: Skill-aware validation frameworks (2% equity, 2:1 reward ratio) continue to fail. qwen35_skill_aware lost -$171.85 on only 4 trades (-$42.96/trade), suggesting oversized positions validated by false-confidence frameworks.

cd3d625cabb6.description: Trade frequency inversely correlates with losses: 0 trades = $0, 4 trades = -$171.85, 8 trades = -$51.45. Even in a bearish market where shorts should profit, active agents lost money.

9e055bf147a3.description: When ALL 5 tracked assets are positive over 24h with gains exceeding +5%, immediately halt ALL SHORT entries regardless of any lower-timeframe signals, validation checks, or risk calculators.

fe00506fffdb.description: Trade frequency must be inversely proportional to regime uncertainty and directly proportional to regime alignment. In this window: 7 trades = +$250.52, 65 trades = +$103.83, 0 trades = $0, 28-30 trades with wrong bias = -$400+.

1872fb1721c5.description: Cap trade frequency at 10 trades/24h maximum in strongly bullish markets when agent directional bias is uncertain. qwen35_skill_aware's 7 trades (+$250.52) vs gptoss_agentic's 28 trades (-$435.19).

e82e9b6d8fdb.description: 2% equity risk with 2:1 reward ratio continues to FAIL when combined with regime misidentification. Sophisticated risk frameworks provide false confidence on wrong-direction trades.

fc1584f7a882.description: Self-reflective position management (journal-aware recognizing overtrading and closing positions) can enable profitability even at high frequency (65 trades, +$103.83), but only when some trades align with actual market direction.

3e20994425b3.description: Cap trade frequency at 16 trades/24h maximum in uniformly bearish markets. Agents with 13-16 trades profited (+$35 to +$50) while agents with 27+ trades lost money (-$6 to -$229).

c683b7a2b371.description: Trade frequency inversely correlates with losses in this bearish window: 0-1 trades = $0, 13-16 trades = +$35-50, 14 trades = -$64, 24-27 trades = -$6 to -$229, 62 trades = -$71. The relationship is non-linear — moderate frequency (13-16) was optimal.

0d1ea6bbc582.description: Self-reflective position management (journal-aware) does NOT guarantee outperformance when trade frequency is very high (62 trades). Meta-cognitive exits were overwhelmed by the volume of wrong-direction entries.

f0625214c593.description: In uniformly bearish markets, SHORT-biased strategies with 26-48 trades/24h can be highly profitable. The sweet spot appears to be 30-48 trades when directional bias is correct.

a3481a34d8a5.description: Low-frequency LONG entries in a bearish market result in outsized per-trade losses. qwen35_simple's 4 trades lost -$99.44 (-$24.86/trade), worse per-trade than higher-frequency agents.

f705cff612ff.description: Self-reflective position management (journal-aware) enables profitable high-frequency trading in bearish markets when directional bias is correct. 48 trades with +$296.01 PnL.

7fe3799f098e.description: Contrarian LONG entries in uniformly bearish markets lose money. gptoss_contrarian's 9 trades lost -$15.04, confirming that fighting the trend is unprofitable.

e7ac99a649a2.description: In uniformly bearish markets with mild declines (<3%), moderate trade frequency (30-32 trades/24h) with SHORT bias outperforms both zero trading and high-frequency approaches. Forum-aware's 31 trades (+$250.04) vs contrarian's 11 trades (-$12.29) vs skill-aware's 27 trades (-$108.44).

135909b96afc.description: Skill-aware validation frameworks (2% equity, 2:1 reward ratio) continue to fail when directional bias is wrong. gptoss_skill_aware lost -$108.44 on 27 trades despite likely using validated entries.

8cff70cc1c14.description: Contrarian LONG entries in bearish markets lose money even at low frequency. gptoss_contrarian's 11 trades lost -$12.29.

9270a61bc18d.description: In mixed/low-volatility markets (daily moves < 0.5%), cap trade frequency at 31 trades/24h maximum. Forum-aware's 24 trades (+$232.17) and agentic's 30 trades (+$123.26) both profited, while momentum's 33 trades lost -$59.65.

8c8351d3886c.description: Avoid momentum-following strategies in mixed/low-volatility markets. When daily moves are under 0.5%, momentum signals generate false entries that whipsaw. gptoss_momentum lost -$59.65 on 33 trades.

059af7b4f5af.description: Contrarian strategies underperform in mixed markets with no clear trend to fade. gptoss_contrarian's 16 trades lost -$38.04 when there was no dominant trend to reverse against.

4617540e2380.description: Forum/social sentiment filtering provides edge in mixed markets by identifying which of the small moves have conviction behind them. gptoss_forum_aware's +$232.17 on 24 trades suggests sentiment signals correctly filtered noise.

87f3b6d203a6.description: When ALL 5 tracked assets are positive over 24h with gains exceeding +2.4%, immediately halt ALL SHORT entries regardless of any lower-timeframe signals, validation checks, or risk calculators.

b10ce41e7710.description: 2% equity risk with 2:1 reward ratio FAILS when combined with regime misidentification. gptoss_skill_aware lost -$340.51 on 31 trades despite using validated sizing frameworks.

660c2ac9439e.description: Self-reflective position management (recognizing overtrading and closing positions) enables profitability even when some entries are wrong-direction. journal_aware's +$55.48 vs skill_aware's -$340.51 at similar trade counts.

fd06e8f64070.description: Cap trade frequency at 15 trades/24h maximum in bullish markets when agent directional bias is uncertain. Momentum's 15 trades (-$25.04) vs skill_aware's 31 trades (-$340.51) shows frequency amplifies losses.

131474e6b57f.description: Contrarian LONG entries in bullish markets should profit, but contrarian agent lost -$22.72 on 6 trades, suggesting the contrarian strategy was actually shorting (fading the rally) rather than going long.
